name: Roast Goose with Garlic, Onion and Sage Stuff
recipeIngredient: Rubbed or ground dried sage (5 1/2 tbsp)
Chicken stock or canned broth (1 cup)
recipeInstructions:
Stuffing:
1. Melt butter in a heavy large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add onions, celery, and garlic; sauté until soft, about 8 minutes.
3. Combine stuffing mixture, sage, salt, oregano, thyme, pepper, and Italian seasoning in a large bowl.
4. Stir in onion mixture and eggs.
5. Add chicken stock or canned broth and mix well.
6. Set aside.
Goose:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
2. Rinse goose inside and out; pat dry with paper towels. Rub the outside of the goose with halved lemon, seasoning it inside and out with salt and pepper.
3. Fill the main cavity and neck cavity loosely with stuffing.
4. Place any remaining stuffing in a small buttered baking dish and cover with foil.
5. Run fingers between breast meat and skin to loosen the skin; place bacon slices under the breast skin.
6. Wrap goose in cheesecloth and place on a rack set into a large roasting pan.
7. Roast goose for 30 minutes at 450°F (230°C).
8. Reduce heat to 350°F (175°C). Continue roasting until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh registers 180°F, basting every 20 minutes with pan juices, about 1 hour and 20 minutes.
9. During the last 40 minutes, place stuffing in the covered baking dish in the oven.
10. Remove cheesecloth; transfer goose to a platter. Pass stuffing separately.
Yield: 6 servings
